      "それは、4つのボードを貫く対角線上の勝利ラインを持つ特徴があります。",
      "それは、数学的に分析された特徴があります。",
      "それは、開発され公開された先手必勝戦略を持つ特徴があります。",
      "それは、ゲームのために販売された市販の装置セットを持つ特徴があります。",

      "それは、ほとんどの人間プレイヤーが記憶して適用するには複雑すぎる先手必勝戦略を持つという特徴があります。",

      "それは、3x3x3バージョンでは、最初のプレイヤーが最初のステップで中央のセルを取ることが妨げられた場合、簡単に後手必勝となるという特徴があります。",
      "それは、3x3x3バージョンでは、中央のセルを完全に禁止することによって、簡単に先手必勝となるという特徴があります。",
      "それは、3人目のプレイヤーを含めることによって、引き分けまで完璧なゲームがプレイされるという特徴があります。",
      "それは、4x4x4ボードの対角線上に16個のセルがあるという特徴があります。",
      "それは、4x4x4ボードの48個のセルが4つの勝利ラインに含まれているという特徴があります。"

About: It is an abstract strategy board game, generally for two players.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with traditional tic-tac-toe.
ConsistsOf: It consists of a cubical array of cells, usually 4×4×4.
HasA: It has players taking turns placing their markers in blank cells in the array.
It has a winning row that can be horizontal, vertical, or diagonal on a single board.
It has a winning row that can be vertical in a column.
It has a winning row that can be a diagonal line through four boards.
It has been analyzed mathematically.
It has a first-player-win strategy that was developed and published.
It has commercial sets of apparatus that have been sold for the game.
It has a 3×3×3 version that cannot end in a draw.
It has a 4×4×4 board with 76 winning lines.
It has a group of automorphisms containing 192 automorphisms.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by being played in a cubical array of cells, usually 4×4×4.
It is characterized by the first player to achieve four of their own markers in a row winning.
It is characterized by being playable with pencil and paper with a hand-drawn board.
It is characterized by a first-player-win strategy that is too complicated for most human players to memorize and apply.
It is characterized by the 3×3×3 version being easily won by the first player unless a rule is adopted that prevents the first player from taking the center cell on his first step.
It is characterized by the 3×3×3 version being easily won by the second player if the first player is prevented from taking the center cell on his first step.
It is characterized by the 3×3×3 version being easily won by the first player by banning the use of the center cell altogether.
It is characterized by the 3×3×3 version being played out to a draw by including a 3rd player.
It is characterized by the 4×4×4 board having 16 cells lying on the body diagonals.
It is characterized by the 4×4×4 board having 48 cells included in four winning lines.
IsA: It is 3D tic-tac-toe.
It is an abstract strategy board game.
It is generally for two players.
It is similar in concept to traditional tic-tac-toe.
It is played in a cubical array of cells, usually 4×4×4.
It is a game that may also be played with pencil and paper with a hand-drawn board.
It is a game for which a first-player-win strategy was developed and published.
It is a game that can be played with pencil and paper.
It is a game that can be played with a hand-drawn board.
It is a game that can be played using the usual "naughts and crosses" to mark moves.
KnownAs: It is known by the trade name Qubic.
It is known as "Parker Brothers 3D Tic Tac Toe Game".
ManufacturedBy: It was manufactured by Parker Brothers, starting in 1964.
It was manufactured by Duplicon in 1947.
UsedFor: It is used for playing a game.
It is used for placing markers in blank cells.
It is used for achieving four markers in a row.
It is used for playing with pencil and paper.
It is used for drawing a game board by hand.
It is used for marking moves with "naughts and crosses".
It is used for playing against a human opponent.
It is used for entering moves numerically.
It is used for responding with moves numerically.
It is used for looking ahead to 12 moves.
UsedIn: It is used in a cubical array of cells.
It is used in traditional tic-tac-toe.
It is used in a game board.
It is used in pencil and paper play.
It is used in a pad of 50 sheets with preprinted game boards.
It is used in the original issue of the game.
It is used in the 1972 reissue of the game.
It is used in the 3×3×3 version of the game.
It is used in the 4×4×4 board.
It is used in computer programs.
UsedTo: It is used to achieve four of their own markers in a row.
It is used to mark their moves.
It is used to play against a human opponent.
It is used to enter moves numerically.
It is used to respond numerically.
It is used to modify its strategy according to their past behavior.
It is used to look for combinations of one or two free cells shared among two or three rows with particular contents.
It is used to play the game.
It is used to play against the program on one of eight different difficulty settings.

About: それは一般的に2人用のアブストラクト戦略ボードゲームです。

ConsistsOf: それは通常4x4x4の立方体状のセル配列で構成されています。
HasA: それは、プレイヤーが交互に自分のマーカーを配列内の空のセルに配置する特徴があります。
それは、単一のボード上で水平、垂直、または対角線上に並べることができる勝利ラインを持つ特徴があります。
それは、列内の垂直方向の勝利ラインを持つ特徴があります。
それは、4つのボードを貫く対角線上の勝利ラインを持つ特徴があります。
それは、数学的に分析された特徴があります。
それは、開発され公開された先手必勝戦略を持つ特徴があります。
それは、ゲームのために販売された市販の装置セットを持つ特徴があります。
それは、引き分けで終わることができない3x3x3バージョンを持つ特徴があります。
それは、76本の勝利ラインを持つ4x4x4ボードを持つ特徴があります。
それは、192個の自己同型を持つ自己同型群を持つ特徴があります。
HasFeatureOf: それは通常4x4x4の立方体状のセル配列でプレイされるという特徴があります。
それは、最初に4つの自分のマーカーを直線に並べたプレイヤーが勝利するという特徴があります。
それは、手描きのボードで鉛筆と紙でプレイできるという特徴があります。
それは、ほとんどの人間プレイヤーが記憶して適用するには複雑すぎる先手必勝戦略を持つという特徴があります。
それは、3x3x3バージョンでは、最初のプレイヤーが最初のステップで中央のセルを取ることを妨げるルールが採用されない限り、簡単に先手必勝となるという特徴があります。
それは、3x3x3バージョンでは、最初のプレイヤーが最初のステップで中央のセルを取ることが妨げられた場合、簡単に後手必勝となるという特徴があります。
それは、3x3x3バージョンでは、中央のセルを完全に禁止することによって、簡単に先手必勝となるという特徴があります。
それは、3人目のプレイヤーを含めることによって、引き分けまで完璧なゲームがプレイされるという特徴があります。
それは、4x4x4ボードの対角線上に16個のセルがあるという特徴があります。
それは、4x4x4ボードの48個のセルが4つの勝利ラインに含まれているという特徴があります。

それは伝統的な三目並べのコンセプトに似ています。
